---
title: "epinowcast & the forum"
description: "Our partner community and where to discuss EpiAware."
---

## epinowcast

[epinowcast](https://www.epinowcast.org) is our partner organisation: a
community of infectious disease modellers, applied epidemiologists, and software
developers building tools for real-time analysis of infectious diseases. Much of
EpiAware grows out of the same people and ideas, now in Julia.

- Website: [epinowcast.org](https://www.epinowcast.org)
- GitHub: [github.com/epinowcast](https://github.com/epinowcast)

## The community forum

The [epinowcast community forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) is the best
place to discuss EpiAware, real-time infectious disease analysis, and the
methods behind the tools. It is a good place to ask questions, share work, and
follow what others are doing — start a topic there rather than emailing if it
might be useful to others.

[Visit the forum ↗](https://community.epinowcast.org/){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}

## Related R packages

The epinowcast ecosystem includes widely used R packages —
[epinowcast](https://github.com/epinowcast/epinowcast),
[EpiNow2](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiNow2),
[primarycensored](https://github.com/epinowcast/primarycensored), and
[scoringutils](https://github.com/epiforecasts/scoringutils) — used by academics
and public health teams internationally. EpiAware brings this work to Julia as
composable components.
